code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a celestial body into a single fluent sentence.
    The function assumes all triples refer to the same subject (the celestial body).
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Use the subject from the first triple (all subjects are expected to be the same)
    subject = triples[0].subject

    # Build a phrase for each predicate‑object pair
    phrases = [f"{t.predicate} {t.object}" for t in triples]

    # Join the phrases into a natural‑language list
    if len(phrases) == 1:
        body = phrases[0]
    else:
        body = ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + phrases[-1]

    # Produce the final sentence
    return f"{subject} has {body}."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

  def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
      """
      Convert a list of triples about a celestial body into a single fluent sentence.
      The function assumes all triples refer to the same subject (the celestial body).
      """
      if not triples:
          return ""
  
      # Use the subject from the first triple (all subjects are expected to be the same)
      subject = triples[0].subject
  
      # Build a phrase for each predicate‑object pair
      phrases = [f"{t.predicate} {t.object}" for t in triples]
  
      # Join the phrases into a natural‑language list
      if len(phrases) == 1:
          body = phrases[0]
      else:
          body = ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + phrases[-1]
  
      # Produce the final sentence
      return f"{subject} has {body}."
